channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump says a deal to end war with Iran is near, but many questions remain
item.description: U.S. and Iran are nearing a tentative deal to end the conflict, reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and curb Tehran's uranium stockpile — though major details remain unresolved.
item.content:encoded: U.S. and Iran are nearing a tentative deal to end the conflict, reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and curb Tehran's uranium stockpile — though major details remain unresolved.
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Kill your lawn, plant native flowers
item.description: Life Kit explains the benfits of swapping your lawn for a native plant garden
item.content:encoded: Life Kit explains the benfits of swapping your lawn for a native plant garden
item.dc:creator: Celia Llopis-Jepsen

item.title: Speed of Ebola outbreak challenges global health workers in wake of U.S. funding cuts
item.description: There have been more than 900 suspected and confirmed cases of Ebola, resulting in more than 180 deaths. It's the first major outbreak the Trump Administration drastically cut health aid programs.
item.content:encoded: There have been more than 900 suspected and confirmed cases of Ebola, resulting in more than 180 deaths. It's the first major outbreak the Trump Administration drastically cut health aid programs.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Has the U.S. lost the war in Iran?
item.description: As both the US and Iran signal a peace deal is near, Robert Kagan, a Senior Fellow at the Brookings Institution, says the U.S. will likely come out weaker than before the war.
item.content:encoded: As both the US and Iran signal a peace deal is near, Robert Kagan, a Senior Fellow at the Brookings Institution, says the U.S. will likely come out weaker than before the war.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Remains of lost arctic explorers identified with DNA, nearly two centuries later
item.description: New research shines a light on the lost Franklin Expedition, a 19th century voyage to the Canadian Arctic gone awry.
item.content:encoded: New research shines a light on the lost Franklin Expedition, a 19th century voyage to the Canadian Arctic gone awry.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Cineplexity: The state of Star Wars
item.description: NPR's Star Wars nerds talk about whether the franchise still has the juice, as 'Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu' hits the cinemas.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Star Wars nerds talk about whether the franchise still has the juice, as 'Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu' hits the cinemas.
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon

item.title: Religious leaders, lawmakers push for $1 billion to secure houses of worship
item.description: There's an effort on Capitol Hill to increase funding for the Nonprofit Security Grant Program, which awards funding to houses of worship to harden their defenses. In 2024, roughly a third of those who applied actually received funding.
item.content:encoded:
There's an effort on Capitol Hill to increase funding for the Nonprofit Security Grant Program, which awards funding to houses of worship to harden their defenses. In 2024, roughly a third of those who applied actually received funding.

(Image credit: Photo courtesy of the Jewish Federations of North America)
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: President Trump says U.S. and Iran close to a deal for ending the war
item.description: President Donald Trump posted to social media on Saturday afternoon that a deal to end the war with Iran "will be announced" shortly.
item.content:encoded: President Donald Trump posted to social media on Saturday afternoon that a deal to end the war with Iran "will be announced" shortly.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: The thorny ethical questions that emerge from covering our place in the natural world
item.description: Nate Rott's beat takes him to some really wild places, asking thorny ethical questions that emerge as he reports on the natural world and humanity's relationship to it
item.content:encoded: Nate Rott's beat takes him to some really wild places, asking thorny ethical questions that emerge as he reports on the natural world and humanity's relationship to it
item.dc:creator: Gabriel J. Sánchez

item.title: The squeeze on Cuba now includes compensation lawsuits
item.description: American University professor William LeoGrande explains how the Supreme Court's decision to allow lawsuits seeking compensation for assets seized in the Cuban revolution to move forward fits in context of current political crisis on the island.
item.content:encoded: American University professor William LeoGrande explains how the Supreme Court's decision to allow lawsuits seeking compensation for assets seized in the Cuban revolution to move forward fits in context of current political crisis on the island.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson
